description: From a place on the ground, 'waero' is the visible expanse above and outward where daylight, weather, moon, and stars appear. It is the encountered sky rather than a scientific synonym for the atmosphere or outer space. The apparent horizon can be called 'waero norui', the sky boundary.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for the 'w' glide, then open into central 'a'. Preserve the hiatus while bringing the tongue forward into stressed middle-height 'e'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge and finish with middle-height rounded 'o'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Weather and direction can be read in the same sky that gives a place its daylight and seasons. Night routes use that field as well. Reliable signs remain local to latitude, terrain, climate, and the knowledge tradition reading them.

semantic_domains.nature: the visible field in which weather and celestial bodies appear
semantic_domains.spatial: the apparent expanse above and around an observer
